message:
fix(scan): do not merge data_dir with home fallback when caller's data_dir is already agent storage

Pre-existing bug in factory.rs and kimi.rs `scan()`: when
`ctx.use_default_detection()` was true AND the explicitly-given
`data_dir` looked like the agent's own session storage, both the
caller-provided path AND the platform home fallback (e.g.
`~/.factory/sessions`) were added to the scan roots. The caller's
explicit intent — "scan this specific archive" — got silently fused
with whatever happened to live under the home default.

This fails two ways:

1. **For library users** who point cass at a specific factory/kimi
   archive (or an alternate mirror), the results get polluted with
   unrelated local agent data, and the caller has no way to ask for
   "just this one location" via `local_default`.
2. **For the test suite**, 36 factory and kimi tests passed on clean
   CI but failed on any machine that actually had Factory/Kimi
   installed, because their tempdir fixtures got silently merged with
   the developer's real agent history. Confirmed empirically: running
   `HOME=/tmp/nonexistent cargo test …scan_parses_session_start_and_messages`
   passes on the same tree where the unset-HOME run fails with
   `left: 17, right: 1` (one test fixture, plus 16 real sessions).

Fix: in both connectors, treat an explicit agent-storage-shaped
`data_dir` as the one-and-only root for that pass. Only fall back
to the home sessions root when `data_dir` is NOT agent storage,
which is the production cass configuration where `data_dir` is
cass's own state directory. No behavior change for that flow.

The existing `path_mapping_boundary_conditions` test gained a
rustfmt rewrap under `cargo fmt` — included in the same commit
since it was incidentally dirty.

All 651 library tests pass (up from 615 passed / 36 failed).
`cargo clippy --features connectors --all-targets -- -D warnings`
clean. `cargo fmt --check` clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -581,7 +581,12 @@ mod conformance {
                 //   Regression: without the drop this produced `"/b//file"`.
                 ("/a", "/b/", "/a/file", Some("/b/file")),
                 //   from no `/`, to no `/`     → to + rest            (rest keeps its sep)
-                ("/a", "/b", "/a/deep/nested/file", Some("/b/deep/nested/file")),
+                (
+                    "/a",
+                    "/b",
+                    "/a/deep/nested/file",
+                    Some("/b/deep/nested/file"),
+                ),
                 // Windows-style backslash flavor of each shape:
                 ("C:\\a", "D:\\b\\", "C:\\a\\file", Some("D:\\b\\file")),
                 ("C:\\a\\", "D:\\b", "C:\\a\\file", Some("D:\\b\\file")),
